title: Dairy Industry (Raw Milk Grading and Minimum Quality and Safety Requirements) Regulations, 2007
title_en: Dairy Industry (Raw Milk Grading and Minimum Quality and Safety Requirements) Regulations, 2007

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These regulations max be cited as "The Dairy Industry (Raw Milk Grading and Minimum quality and safety requirements) Regulations. 2007" and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These regulations shall apply to all areas in which the Act applies.

provisions.title: Interpreta­tion
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires: " Act " means the Dairy Industry Act . 2004; " ADI " means an Acceptable Daily Intake of a drug which during an entire life time, appears to be without appreciable risk to the health of consumer on the basis of all the known facts at the time of the evaluation of the drug residue in the milk and milk products. " Approved premises " shall mean a dairy registered by the Board for the purpose of production, manufacturing, storage, handling or sale of milk or milk products. " Board " means Tanzania Dairy Board established under section 8 of the Act ; " Container " means any box. package, detachable compartment or receptacle or any other arrangement in which milk max be carried by or on behalf of a milk dealer to any place whether for sale or otherwise and such containers shall be approved by the Board ; " Dairy (arm " means any place or premises where daily animal are kept for producing milk; " Dairy " means any premises used for the production, processing or manufacturing of milk into daily products for sale; " Grader " means a person appointed as an inspector, or person recognized and authorized by the Board to earn' out the function of grading of milk and dairy products: " Maximum residue limits for pesticides (MR1. P) " means the maximum concentration of a pesticide expressed in mg/kg permitted in milk and milk products by the Board ; " Maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs (MRI. VD) " means-the maximum concentration of the residue resulting from the use of the veterinary drugs, expressed in mg/kg or ug/kg on a fresh basis that is specified by the Board : " Milk sample " means a sample of milk ordain product that is collected for the purpose of the Act and these regulations. " Minister " means Minister responsible for Livestock Development; "Potable water" means water fit for human consumption, the standard of which should not be lower than that prescribed by the Tanzania Bureau of Standards: " Raw milk " means the normal mammary secretion of dairy animals obtained from healthy animal one or more milking without cither addition to it or extraction from it and intended for consumption as liquid milk or further processing; " Tanzania Standards " means a national standard published under the Tanzania Standards Act . 1975: Part II – Grading of raw material

provisions.title: Conditions for sale of raw milk
provisions.content: (1) No person shall sell raw milk unless the milk has passed platform tests as shown in the First Schedule). (2) Any milk which contains substances other than milk components shall be termed as adulterated milk.

provisions.title: Weighing and grading of raw milk
provisions.content: Every shipment of raw milk supplied by a producer shall be weighed and graded as prescribed in the first Schedule to these regulations

provisions.title: Procedure for ­dealing with rejected milk
provisions.content: (1) Any milk which does not pass the platform test shall be rejected by the grader. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions under sub regulation (1) a grader shall reject milk that (a) comes from an animal 14 days before and not less than 5 days after parturition, or any longer periods that arc necessary to ensure that the milk is free from colostrums: (b) contains blood, coagulation or other foreign particles; (c) has odours that adversely affect its organoleptic characteristics (3) A grader who rejects milk or at a dairy pursuant to the provisions of sub-regulation (1) shall— (a) attach to the container a tag showing the reason for rejection: (b) add a harmless colouring matter: and (c) notify the person who supplied the milk that the milk has been rejected and shall be disposed in accordance with the regulations made under the Act .

provisions.title: Equipments and facilities for milk testing
provisions.content: (1) Every milk collecting premises shall have (a) a lest room or place approved by the Board for testing milk; (b) equipment and other facilities for testing milk for fat. density, pH, or any other parameters as prescribed in the Tanzania Standards (2) A grader shall verify that containers for milk samples are clearly identified and tightly stoppered or sealed with other acceptable closure and kept at temperatures not exceeding 10 o C

provisions.title: Sampling milk for microbiological tests
provisions.content: (1) Grader shall ensure that equipment used for sampling milk for microbiological quality control are sterilized at least al 120°C for 15 minutes. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub regulation (1) of this regulation sampling of milk for bacteriological tests shall be carried out in accordance with existing Tanzania Standards.

provisions.title: Adulterated raw milk
provisions.content: Raw milk shall be adulterated if water, preservatives other than those permitted under the Tanzania Standards for permissible food additives or am other substances that have been added in the milk or by subtraction from milk any component and replacing it with other substances Part III – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Restriction on dealing with raw milk
provisions.content: (1) No person shall sell or distribute to the public any milk for human consumption unless such milk has been pasteurized, sterilized or subjected to such treatment to render it safe for human consumption. (2) Persons handling raw milk shall be routinely medically examined clean in person and habits and shall put on protective clothing. (3) No person shall sell raw milk containing drug residues above levels indicated in the Second Schedules of these regulations or am harmful chemical residues including pesticide residues above levels indicated in the and Third Schedule of these regulations or mix other chemical above levels as prescribed by the Board . (4) No person shall sell raw milk produced from an animal which is or has been tinder treatment before the withdrawal period of the drug used has passed (5) No person shall sell and distribute raw milk with microbiological quality specifications other than those specified in these regulations

provisions.title: Board to prepare Codes of Practices
provisions.content: The Board shall prepare Codes of Practices to be used by stakeholders for the purpose of enhancing milk quality and safely

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any person who (a) willfully delays or obstructs any grader in the exercise of his powers under these regulations; or (b) refuses or fails without reasonable cause to give any information which he is lawfully required to give under these regulations: or (c) gives am information which is false in a material particular or which he reasonably knows to be untrue; or (d) distributes, offers, or presents for sale, stores, dispatches to any other persons for the purpose of sale or distribute for human consumption or helps any other person in doing the same contrary to the provisions of these regulations shall be guilt) of an offence. (2) Any person, who is found guilty of an offence under these Regulations, shall upon conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding four months or to both such fine and imprisonment

provisions.title: Revocations and savings
provisions.content: Any regulations, rules, orders or notices made under the Dairy Industry Act . 1965 which provides for matters provided under these Regulations are hereby revoked First Schedule (Made under regulation 1 and 5) Platform and other quality tests for raw milk Platform tests Characteristics Grade 1 Grade 2 Grade 3 Grade 4 Density(CLR) gm/cc at at 20 o C I.027 1.033 1. 025 < 1.027 1.024 < 1.025 <1.024 Odour Normal milk odour Normal milk odour Slightly off odour - fodder, cow dung Off odour - fodder, cow dung, other smells Colour Normal milk colour - White or light cream colour Normal milk colour - White or light cream colour Slight abnormal colour reddish Abnormal milk Alcohol Precipitation Test (APT) Negative 72 % Negative 68 % Negative 68 % Positive 68 % Clot on Boiling (COB) Negative Negative Negative Positive Composition tests Fat % >3.5 3.25 < 3.5 2.5 < 3 25 < 2.5 Freezing point o C - U.55 to - 0.525 - 0.524 to-0.510 -0. 509 to 0.5U0 Above 0.19 Microbiological Characteristics pH 6.6 - 6.8 6.4 - 6.5 6.2. - 6.3 <6.2 Acidity 0.14 - 0.18 0 19 - 0 20 0.2I - 6.22 > 0.22 Rezasurin test > 4.0 3.5 - 2.0 2.0 - 1.0 0.5 - 0.0 Methylene Blue test (Reduction time - hours) > 5 3 <5 l<3 <1 Total plate count (Colony Forming Units (CFU) ml <200 00 200 000-1000000 1 000 000 – 500000 > 5 000 000 Coliform Absent in 1400 dilution [NB: Raw milk falling under grades 1 to 3 for further processing besides pasteurization Grade 4 milk may not be used for processing into high quality products like fermented and long-life pasteurized milk.] Second Schedules (Made under Regulation 10) Veterinary drugs residues in milk Substance ADI (ug/kg hw) MRI.
